_note: THROWAWAY PREVIEW ONLY — the illustrative sample fixture is temporarily replaced with the REAL, corrected EQL manifest (from cipherstash/encrypt-query-language, incl. the #372 mis-parse fix) so the Vercel preview renders the full generated functions catalog. DO NOT MERGE.
name: eql

functions.name: ciphertext
functions.brief: Extract ciphertext (c) from a raw jsonb encrypted value.

functions.name: eq_term
functions.brief: XOR-aware equality term extractor for public.jsonb_entry.
functions.description: Returns the bytea of whichever deterministic term the sv entry carries — hm (HMAC-256) or oc (CLLW ORE). The two byte distributions are disjoint by construction, so byte equality on the coalesce is unambiguous. Canonical equality extractor used by = / <> on jsonb_entry.

returns.description: bytea Decoded hm or oc bytes (NULL if entry is NULL).

functions.name: eql_v3_is_valid_ste_vec_document_payload
functions.brief: Validate a root SteVec document payload.

functions.name: eql_v3_is_valid_ste_vec_entry_payload
functions.brief: Validate a single SteVec entry payload.

functions.name: eql_v3_is_valid_ste_vec_query_payload
functions.brief: Validate a SteVec containment query payload.

functions.name: has_ore_cllw
functions.brief: Check if a ste_vec entry contains a CLLW ORE index term.

returns.description: boolean True if oc is present and non-null.

functions.name: jsonb_array
functions.brief: Extract deterministic search fields (s, hm, oc, op) per sv element.
functions.description: Excludes non-deterministic ciphertext so PostgreSQL's native jsonb @> can compare for containment. Use for GIN indexes and containment queries.

returns.description: jsonb[] Array of objects with only deterministic fields.

functions.name: jsonb_array_elements
functions.brief: Extract elements of an encrypted JSONB array as rows.

params.name: val
params.description: encrypted EQL payload (must have a flag true).

returns.description: SETOF public.jsonb_entry One row per element (metadata preserved).

throws: 'cannot extract elements from non-array' if not an array.

functions.name: jsonb_array_elements_text
functions.brief: Extract elements of an encrypted JSONB array as ciphertext text.

params.name: val
params.description: encrypted EQL payload (must have a flag true).

returns.description: SETOF text One ciphertext per element.

throws: 'cannot extract elements from non-array' if not an array.

functions.name: jsonb_array_length
functions.brief: Get the length of an encrypted JSONB array.

params.name: val
params.description: encrypted EQL payload (must have a flag true).

throws: 'cannot get array length of a non-array' if not an array.

functions.name: jsonb_contained_by

returns.description: boolean True if all elements of a are contained in b.

functions.notes: Public raw-jsonb[] reverse-containment helper — the function-form entrypoint for <@ on platforms without operator support. The typed public.json <@ operator binds to eql_v3.ste_vec_contains instead, but both agree on the result.

functions.name: jsonb_contains
functions.brief: GIN-indexable containment check: does a contain all of b?

returns.description: boolean True if a contains all deterministic elements of b.

functions.notes: Public raw-jsonb[] containment helper over the extracted deterministic fields — the function-form entrypoint for containment on platforms without operator support (Supabase/PostgREST). The typed public.json @> operator does NOT call this function — it binds to eql_v3.ste_vec_contains instead — but both agree on the result (a parity test pins this). Also the documented GIN index expression (eql_v3.jsonb_array(col)); see docs/reference/database-indexes.md.

functions.name: jsonb_path_exists
functions.brief: Check if a selector path exists in encrypted JSONB.

returns.description: boolean True if a matching element exists.

functions.name: jsonb_path_query
functions.brief: Query encrypted JSONB for sv elements matching .
functions.description: Returns one jsonb_entry row per matching encrypted element. Returns empty set on no match. It deliberately does not wrap multiple matches as an public.json document, because the root document domain requires an sv array and single leaves belong to public.jsonb_entry.

functions.name: jsonb_path_query_first
functions.brief: Get the first sv element matching , or NULL.

returns.description: public.jsonb_entry First matching element or NULL.
public.jsonb_entry The entry with the largest CLLW ORE term.

functions.name: meta_data
functions.brief: Extract metadata (i, v) from a raw jsonb encrypted value.

returns.description: jsonb Metadata object with i and v fields.
public.jsonb_entry The entry with the smallest CLLW ORE term.

functions.name: ore_cllw
functions.brief: Extract CLLW ORE index term from a ste_vec entry.
functions.description: oc is only ever present on an sv element, never at a root encrypted value, so the typed overload accepts public.jsonb_entry. Returns SQL NULL when oc is absent (btree NULL-filters such rows from range queries).

returns.description: eql_v3_internal.ore_cllw Composite carrying the CLLW ciphertext, or NULL.

functions.name: selector
functions.brief: Extract selector (s) from a raw jsonb encrypted value.

functions.name: selector
functions.brief: Extract selector (s) from a ste_vec entry. The DOMAIN CHECK guarantees s is present, so this is a simple field access.

functions.name: ste_vec
functions.brief: Extract the sv element array as raw jsonb[].
functions.description: Returns the elements of sv (or a single-element array wrapping the value when there is no sv). No envelope re-wrapping — raw jsonb elements.

functions.name: ste_vec_contains
functions.brief: Does encrypted value a contain all sv elements of b?
functions.description: Empty b is always contained. Each element of b must match selector + eq_term in some element of a.

returns.description: boolean True if all elements of b are contained in a.

functions.name: ste_vec_contains
functions.brief: Check if an sv array contains a specific sv element.
functions.description:
Match = selector equal AND eq_term equal (byte-equality over coalesce(hm, oc)). This collapses the v2 hm/oc CASE: under the XOR contract both terms are deterministic and byte-disjoint, so either one is a valid equality discriminator and a single byte comparison is correct.

ASSUMPTION (locked by a negative test in v3_jsonb_tests.rs): hm and oc byte distributions never collide at a given selector. The crypto layer configures a selector for eq XOR ordered, so both sides of a real comparison carry the same term type; and an oc value carries a leading domain-tag byte an hm never has. Unlike v2's explicit has_hmac(both)/has_ore_cllw(both)/ELSE false CASE, this collapse would wrongly match an hm needle against an oc leaf if their hex bytes were ever identical — which the contract prevents. The negative-containment test guards against regression.

returns.description: boolean True if b is found in any element of a.

functions.name: to_ste_vec_query
functions.brief: Convert a public.json to a jsonb_query needle.
functions.description: Normalises each sv element down to the matching-relevant fields: s plus exactly one of hm / oc. Other fields (c, a, i/v, anything else) are stripped. This is the canonical needle shape for @> containment. Designed for use as a functional GIN index expression: GIN (eql_v3.to_ste_vec_query(col)::jsonb jsonb_path_ops).

returns.description: public.jsonb_query Query-shaped needle, sv elements normalised.

functions.name: version
functions.brief: Get the installed EQL version string.
functions.description: Returns the version string for the installed EQL library. This value is baked in at build time from the release tag.

returns.description: text Version string (e.g. "3.0.0" or "DEV" for development builds)

functions.name: bloom_filter
functions.brief: Extract the Bloom-filter index term from a jsonb payload.
functions.description: Inlinable single-statement SQL — the planner can fold this into the calling query so the functional GIN index built on eql_v3_internal.match_term(col) (which calls this) engages structurally. Mirrors eql_v3_internal.hmac_256(jsonb): no RAISE and no pinned search_path. Returns NULL when bf is absent or present but not a json array, rather than raising. The domain CHECK guarantees the bf key is present but not that it is an array, so a non-array bf (e.g. {"bf": null}) can reach here even on a typed value; gating on jsonb_typeof(...) = 'array' returns NULL for that case — and for raw jsonb outside the domain — instead of erroring inside . NULL, like the HMAC extractor, is the right answer. An empty bf array yields an empty filter (contains nothing, contained by everything), matching set-containment semantics.

returns.description: eql_v3_internal.bloom_filter The bf array as a smallint[] domain value, or NULL when bf is absent or not a json array.

functions.name: compare_ore_block_256_term
functions.brief: Compare two ORE block terms using cryptographic comparison.

returns.type: CREATE OPERATOR CLASS eql_v3_internal ore_block_256_operator_class DEFAULT FOR TYPE eql_v3_internal ore_block_256 USING btree FAMILY eql_v3_internal ore_block_256_operator_family AS OPERATOR public<, OPERATOR 2 public.<=, OPERATOR 3 public.=, OPERATOR 4 public. > OPERATOR FUNCTION eql_v3_internal

functions.name: compare_ore_block_256_terms
functions.brief: Compare arrays of ORE block terms recursively.

functions.name: encrypted_domain_unsupported_bool
functions.brief: Shared blocker helper. Raises 'operator X is not supported for TYPE' so unsupported domain operators surface a clear error rather than fall through to native jsonb behaviour.

functions.name: encrypted_domain_unsupported_jsonb
functions.brief: Shared blocker helper returning jsonb. Identical to encrypted_domain_unsupported_bool but typed for blockers shadowing native operators whose result is jsonb (#>, -, #-, ||), so composed expressions resolve and the body raises rather than failing earlier with a misleading 'operator does not exist' on a boolean result.

functions.name: encrypted_domain_unsupported_text
functions.brief: Shared blocker helper returning text. Identical to encrypted_domain_unsupported_bool but typed for blockers shadowing the native #>> operator whose result is text.

functions.name: has_bloom_filter
functions.brief: Test whether a jsonb payload carries a Bloom-filter (bf) term.

returns.description: boolean True when the bf key is present and non-null.

functions.name: has_hmac_256
functions.brief: Check if JSONB payload contains HMAC-SHA256 index term.

returns.description: boolean True if 'hm' field is present and non-null

functions.name: has_ore_block_256
functions.brief: Check if JSONB payload contains an ORE block index term.

returns.description: boolean True only if the 'ob' field is present and is a JSON array

functions.notes: A well-formed ORE index term is always a JSON array of block terms, so this guard treats a present-but-non-array ob (a scalar or object) as absent. That makes the extractor RAISE on a structurally invalid ob payload at the boundary instead of silently degrading it to a NULL index term in . The previous val ->> 'ob' IS NOT NULL form stringified scalars/objects and so reported them as present. {} (absent ob) and {"ob": null} (JSON null) both remain false.

functions.name: has_ore_cllw
functions.brief: Check if a raw jsonb value contains a CLLW ORE index term.

params.name: val
params.description: An object that may carry an oc field

returns.description: boolean True if oc field is present and non-null

returns.description: eql_v3_internal.hmac_256 HMAC-SHA256 hash value, or NULL when hm is absent

functions.name: ope_cllw
functions.brief: Extract CLLW OPE index term from JSONB payload.
functions.description:
Returns the CLLW OPE ciphertext from the op field of an encrypted scalar payload, hex-decoded to the bytea-backed eql_v3_internal.ope_cllw domain.

Inlinable single-statement SQL — the body is a strict expression of the argument (->> and decode are both STRICT), so the planner folds this into the calling query and functional btree indexes built on eql_v3.ord_ope_term(col) (which calls this) engage structurally, the same way the hmac_256 equality chain does.

Missing-op semantics: val ->> 'op' is NULL when op is absent and the strict chain propagates it, so the extractor returns SQL NULL and btree's NULL handling filters those rows from range queries.

returns.description: eql_v3_internal.ope_cllw Hex-decoded CLLW OPE term, or NULL when op is absent

functions.name: ore_block_256
functions.brief: Extract ORE block index term from JSONB payload.

functions.name: ore_block_256_eq
functions.brief: Equality backing function for ORE block types.

functions.name: ore_block_256_gt
functions.brief: Greater-than backing function for ORE block types.

functions.name: ore_block_256_gte
functions.brief: Greater-than-or-equal backing function for ORE block types.

functions.name: ore_block_256_lt
functions.brief: Less-than backing function for ORE block types.

functions.name: ore_block_256_lte
functions.brief: Less-than-or-equal backing function for ORE block types.

functions.name: ore_block_256_neq
functions.brief: Not-equal backing function for ORE block types.

functions.name: ore_cllw
functions.brief: Extract CLLW ORE index term from raw jsonb.
functions.description:
Returns the CLLW ORE ciphertext from the oc field of a single sv element supplied as raw jsonb. Inlinable single-statement SQL — the planner folds the body into the calling query.

Missing-oc semantics: returns SQL-level NULL (not a composite with NULL bytes) when oc is absent, so btree's NULL handling filters those rows from range queries.

params.name: val
params.description: An object carrying an oc field

returns.description: eql_v3_internal.ore_cllw Composite carrying the CLLW ciphertext, or NULL when the oc field is absent.

functions.name: owned_schemas
functions.brief: Schemas owned by the eql_v3 surface.
functions.description: Single source of truth for tooling that must enumerate every schema this installer owns (eql_v3.lints(), tasks/pin_search_path_v3.sql), so a future third eql_v3-family schema is one array literal to edit instead of a hardcoded schema-name predicate repeated at every call site. Keep in sync with the SCHEMA / INTERNAL_SCHEMA constants in crates/eql-codegen/src/consts.rs — those drive what codegen emits into each schema; this drives what tooling scans across both.

returns.description: name[] The schema names eql_v3 owns (public + internal).
